findings.title: New EmbeddedConfig sampler setters are untested
findings.impact: The PR adds public embedded-worker configuration setters that silently accept valid rates and ignore invalid, NaN, or out-of-range values, but the diff adds no test that these setters emit the expected request_sample_rate/access_sample_rate/diagnostic_sample_rate args or preserve prior config on invalid input. A regression here would leave embedded deployments unable to configure the new sampler or accidentally accept bad rates without a failing test.
findings.fix: Add focused EmbeddedConfig tests covering all three new setters, chaining behavior, exact generated arg names/values, boundary values 0.0 and 1.0, and rejection of negative, >1.0, NaN, and infinity inputs.

findings.title: Heartbeat-delivered sampler config has no regression test
findings.impact: The new client heartbeat path applies LogSampleConfigPb updates after registration, but the added ST only verifies the initial register-delivered snapshot. If heartbeat responses stop carrying or applying updated sampling config, long-lived clients will keep stale sampler state and this PR's tests would still pass.
findings.fix: Add a test that connects a client, changes or injects a heartbeat LogSampleConfigPb with different ppm values, waits for the heartbeat path, and asserts the client LogSampler snapshot changes; cover both missing-field keeps-current and invalid-field retains-previous-good behavior if practical.
